benchmark_details.name: Ambiguous Entity Retrieval (AmbER)
benchmark_details.overview: We introduce AmbER sets, a benchmark for evaluating the entity disambiguation capabilities of retrievers across multiple NLP tasks. Each AmbER set is a collection of queries about entities that share a name, helping assess the role of entity popularity in disambiguation.

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: To evaluate the entity disambiguation capabilities of retrievers in open-domain NLP tasks.
purpose_and_intended_users.limitations: The pipeline relies on Wikipedia and Wikidata, which may result in ambiguous queries if a property is missing from Wikidata.

data.annotation: Automatically generated through a pipeline designed to create AmbER sets.

methodology.calculation: Metrics calculated as the percentage of retrieved documents that are correct.
methodology.interpretation: Higher accuracy indicates better entity disambiguation capabilities of the retrievers.
methodology.validation: Validated through systematic studies on retrieval systems with varying performance on heads vs. tails.
